networks.name: EARMA
networks.full: European Association of Research Managers and Administrators

scans.news: EARMA opened its 2026 Mentorship Scheme, pairing ten mentors with ten mentees from the community; the mentee/mentor application window is open with a kick-off event tied to the programme and an outcome notification expected by mid-October. The EARMA Academy also opened pre-registration for its entry-level RM1 certification assessment, aimed at professionals with 0-2 years of experience.

events.title: Preparing for the Future of AI in Research Management (AI Thematic Group webinar)
Science Communication in Horizon Europe: From Proposal to Impact
Joining the Dots: The Collaborative Power of Research Management (BESTPRAC Thematic Group event)
From Overwhelmed to in Control: Mastering AI in Research Management (masterclass)
Shaping Research Impact Practice in a Changing Landscape (EARMAimpact Thematic Group event)

networks.name: ERRIN
networks.full: European Regions Research and Innovation Network

scans.news: On 13 July, ERRIN submitted formal input to the EU's call for evidence on a future Single Basic Act for Joint Undertakings, arguing that regional and local authorities should be recognised as strategic partners rather than mere implementers. ERRIN also contributed to the Commission's consultation on the Industrial Accelerator Act, and published its 2026 Annual Work Plans across its cluster working groups. Note: ERRIN's events calendar is very high-volume (hundreds of member and working-group meetings); this digest lists only the more flagship/cross-network items rather than every internal session.

events.title: Food Tech Innovations: Connecting Europe's Living Labs - final conference
Annual I3 Instrument Forum 2026 - registration open
European Week of Regions and Cities 2026

scans.news: ESEIA welcomed Vaasa University of Applied Sciences (VAMK) as a new member following recent member-acquisition meetings, expanding the Alliance's presence in the Nordic region. ESEIA also published its 60th newsletter edition and quarterly updates on its EMERGE (Africa green energy transition) and INITIATE Horizon Europe projects.

events.title: 19th ESEIA Lecture Series: Energy System Modelling for Green Africa
Conference for Sustainable Energy (CSE 2026) - paper deadline extended to 31 July
7th ESEIA International Conference: Green Energy Solutions

networks.name: EURASHE
networks.full: European Association of Institutions in Higher Education

scans.news: EURASHE moved its newsletter to a quarterly schedule (January, April, July, October) and, together with partner higher-education associations, published proposed amendments to the next Erasmus+ Programme regulation (2028-2034), pushing to strengthen mobility and cooperation funding. The association also opened its new Brussels office, Applied Sciences House, in the European Quarter, and its 35th Annual Conference in Amsterdam (17-18 June) drew a record 250 participants.

events.title: Higher Education Transformation Week (showcasing Erasmus+ KA2 project results)
EQAF 2026 - call for contributions open (quality assurance forum)

networks.name: EUREGHA
networks.full: European Regional and Local Health Authorities

scans.news: EUREGHA published its 2025 Activity Report, highlighting three new EU-funded projects launched during the year: ENKORE (circular healthcare), YARN (young cancer survivors) and PREDI-LYNCH (Lynch Syndrome prevention). Notably, VAMK led a workshop session on crisis leadership and training at EUREGHA's Annual General Assembly 2026 in Bilbao (27-29 May), alongside sessions from Aarhus Kommune, JOGG and others.

events.title: Next CoR Interregional Group on Health & Well-being meeting (autumn session, exact date not yet published)
events.dateLabel: Date to be confirmed - check euregha.net/calendar closer to the time

networks.name: EVBB
networks.full: European Association of Institutes for Vocational Training

scans.news: EVBB, as lead of the FuTourAlliance project, published a Request for Proposals for external evaluation services. VET4EU2, the joint VET-provider platform that includes EVBB, welcomed former EU Commissioner Ylva Johansson's appointment as chair of the European Commission's Skills High-Level Board. No further upcoming EVBB-organised events are listed on their events page at this scan (Policy Camp 2026 already took place 29 June-3 July).

EVTA ran a three-day CoVE Design Programme seminar in Granada, developed in response to growing member interest in the Erasmus+ Centres of Vocational Excellence funding line, covering the policy rationale, technical call requirements, and partner-matching for future proposals.
